name: Carter 2019 Sildenafil Hypoxia Exercise Systematic Review Extract
population_or_context: Healthy human participants in randomized sildenafil-placebo hypoxia studies.
sample_size: 14 studies; 210 subjects received sildenafil 40, 50, or 100 mg/day.
methods: Systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized placebo-controlled crossover studies through August 2018.
population_profile: Healthy humans under hypoxia; relevant to altitude hypotheses but not tadalafil exposure.
tadalafil_exposure: No tadalafil exposure; sildenafil 40, 50, or 100 mg/day in included studies.
indication_context: Adjacent PDE5 exercise-performance evidence under hypoxia.
study_design: Systematic review/meta-analysis of randomized crossover trials.
safety_findings: Detailed adverse-event synthesis not extracted for this baseline; sildenafil is a shorter-acting PDE5 inhibitor with its own safety profile.
directness_to_target_population: Low to moderate for healthy hypoxia physiology, low for tadalafil because the agent is sildenafil and effects may depend on dose, timing, and altitude condition.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Agent mismatch, heterogeneity in hypoxia type and dose, small trials, and limited transfer to normoxic gym use.
class_transfer_limits: Adjacent sildenafil evidence only; tadalafil's longer half-life, dose timing, and agent-specific pharmacology prevent direct transfer.
